danger_signals.visual_form: Animated sprites of race cars, dune buggies, trucks, and bulldozers moving horizontally across screen in lanes
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate lethal threat; collision results in death (skull and crossbones indicator)

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated alligator sprite with visibly open jaws
danger_signals.meaning: Predatory threat; jumping into open mouth causes death

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated snake sprite on river logs
Animated otter sprite on river logs
Turtle sprite that descends/sinks into water
danger_signals.meaning: Sinking platform hazard; standing on diving turtle causes drowning death
Fall-through zone; jumping into river without platform causes death

danger_signals.visual_form: Numerical timer display at top of screen counting down from 60 or 30
danger_signals.meaning: Time pressure threat; reaching zero before reaching home causes death

danger_signals.visual_form: Frog home space already containing another frog or alligator
danger_signals.meaning: Invalid landing zone; jumping into occupied space causes death

safety_signals.visual_form: Outlined rectangular spaces at top of screen (5 total); at least one always visibly open/empty
safety_signals.meaning: Goal location; successfully reaching home scores 50 points and completes frog objective

safety_signals.visual_form: Horizontal brown/tan colored sprites moving across river area
safety_signals.meaning: Safe riding surface; standing on moving log or turtle prevents drowning

safety_signals.visual_form: Alligator sprite with mouth closed/not visibly open
safety_signals.meaning: Rideable platform; can safely stand on closed-mouth alligator back

safety_signals.visual_form: Horizontal safe zone between road and river sections
safety_signals.meaning: Rest area with no active hazards; temporary safety location

safety_signals.visual_form: Brightly colored frog sprite (distinct visual appearance from player frog) on a log
safety_signals.meaning: Bonus objective; carrying to home scores 200 points

safety_signals.visual_form: Insect sprite in center frog home
safety_signals.meaning: Bonus collectible; eating fly scores 200 points

faction_indicators.color_identity: Green (player frog) and brightly colored variant (female frog, possibly pink/magenta)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Frog sprite shape; five homes at top of screen serve as faction base
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Five frog homes at top of screen represent faction territory; filled homes show active members

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Vehicle sprites: race cars, dune buggies, trucks, bulldozers
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Different vehicle types moving in same direction lanes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Bottom half of screen (road section); horizontal lane structure

faction_indicators.color_identity: Blue (water), browns/tans (logs and turtle), reptile tones (alligators)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Closed-mouth vs open-mouth alligators; diving vs non-diving turtles
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Upper half of screen (river section); horizontal log/creature lane structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen displays full road section, median strip, and river; five empty frog homes visible at top; timer displays 60 seconds; one frog positioned at bottom of screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vehicles move in regular pattern; river creatures move in horizontal lanes; frog is stationary awaiting player input

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Frog sprite moves incrementally up screen; timer counts down continuously; vehicles and river creatures animate in their lanes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Traffic maintains consistent speed and direction; river platforms continue horizontal movement; no pause in hazard behavior

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Frog sprite replaced by skull and crossbones symbol at location of death; timer resets to 60 seconds; visual feedback depends on death type (splat for vehicles, sink for water, etc.)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Music cue or sound effect; brief pause; new frog appears at bottom; life counter decreases visually

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Frog sprite enters home space; home becomes visibly 'filled'; timer resets; score increases on display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Musical note or positive sound effect; new empty frog appears at bottom of screen; difficulty may subtly increase

world_state_to_visual.world_state: All five frogs reach home (level complete)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All five home spaces filled; level counter advances; screen refreshes with new configuration
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Celebratory music; 1,000 point bonus awarded; difficulty increases; traffic/river patterns change

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Timer counts down visibly to final seconds; may flash or change color/intensity to show urgency
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Frog still controllable; hazards continue normal behavior; if timer reaches zero before home, death occurs

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen displays 'GAME OVER' or returns to title/attract mode; final score displayed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Music stops or plays game-over theme; player loses control; high score may be saved/displayed

environmental_storytelling.example: Road section at bottom with multiple vehicle lanes moving in alternating directions creates implicit narrative of 'dangerous urban environment'; river section above with predatory creatures and sinking platforms suggests 'wild nature equally dangerous'; progression through 5 levels with increasing difficulty implies escalating environmental hostility

Five frog homes positioned at top of screen as distinct goal zones; always having at least one open home visually communicates 'objective is achievable but competitive'; filled homes show progress and provide sense of escalating challenge as fewer options remain

Female frog appears randomly on logs in river; fly appears in center home; both are visually distinct and bonus-valued, communicating 'nature provides rare opportunities for extra gain' alongside base survival objective

Skull and crossbones symbol replacing frog sprite at death location immediately communicates 'permanent failure' and 'danger manifest'; different death contexts (vehicle, water, time, occupancy) all use same symbol, creating unified danger language

Safe zone between road and river visually implies 'natural world has rest points'; this small island of safety in sea of hazards creates rhythm of danger-safety-danger that structures the play experience and environmental storytelling

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Continuous horizontal movement in lanes at consistent speed; vehicles wrap around screen edges and respawn; never stop or pause regardless of frog position
Continuous horizontal movement across river in set lanes; move opposite direction from vehicles in adjacent lanes; carry frog with them if frog stands on surface
Horizontal movement like logs; frog can stand and be carried; alligator does not attack while closed mouth
Horizontal movement; mouth position signals active threat; contact with open mouth causes frog death; implies alligator is 'hunting' or 'attacking'
Horizontal movement; some turtles periodically dive underwater; frog standing on diving turtle will sink and drown; sinking behavior signals imminent platform loss
Positioned on logs in river; does not move; contact with snake sprite causes frog death; implies passive danger or alert predator
Moves on river logs; contact causes death; behavior and placement suggest active hunting threat in water environment
Moves one grid space per joystick input in four directions; continuous timer runs down; death resets frog position; reaching home fills that space and spawns new frog at start

notes: Frogger uses a simple but effective visual language: distinct spatial zones (road, median, river) each with characteristic hazards create environmental storytelling through area design. The skull-and-crossbones death indicator is a universal danger signal. Color differentiation (green frog, vehicle colors, blue water, brown platforms) creates visual clarity critical to the arcade puzzle paradigm. Timer visibility and countdown rhythm communicate time pressure as a primary world-state condition. The game's famous 'most ways to die' design is visually expressed through hazard variety and environmental complexity—different visual scenarios (vehicle vs. alligator vs. time) communicate contextually different failure modes. The female frog and fly serve as rare visual rewards that add narrative depth: frog chivalry goal and insect sustenance goal add implicit world-context beyond pure survival.
